name: G2→G3 Zone 2 — The Dead Heart Wandering Monsters
description: Wandering monster table for Zone 2 (Days 3–4): Interior mountain passes. Nothing permanent lives here. What passes through is moving between one place and another — and so is the party. Check frequency: once per 8 hours of travel, once per 8 hours of rest.

results.text: 2d4 Stone Giants (traveling north toward G2; neutral; suspicious) [PF2e Lvl 9 each]
1d4 Hill Giants (G1 stragglers heading south toward fire giant territory) [PF2e Lvl 7 each]
1 Frost Giant Scout (moving fast; carries a sealed report or message tube) [PF2e Lvl 9]
1d6 Barghests (pack; hunting anything that moves) [PF2e Lvl 4 each]
1d3 Chimeras (nested in the high crags; territorial) [PF2e Lvl 8 each]
1 Duergar Patrol — 8 duergar with 1 duergar commander (mercenary supply run toward G3) [PF2e Lvl 2 / Lvl 6]
1 Fire Giant Messenger (moving north toward G2 with a report; alone; formidable) [PF2e Lvl 10]
2d6 Gnoll Hunters (scavenging the dead road) [PF2e Lvl 2 each]
1d4 Basilisks (cluster near a collapsed section; petrified travelers scattered around) [PF2e Lvl 5 each]
1 Roc (nesting on a high peak; party is prey-sized if visible from above) [PF2e Lvl 13 — flee]
1d3 Cloud Giants (traveling; not hostile; find the party amusing) [PF2e Lvl 11 each]
HAZARD — Mountain gale: all movement halved; ranged attacks impossible; DC 18 Athletics or pushed 10 feet per round; lasts 2d4 hours
1 Drow Courier Party (4 drow moving between G2 and G3; will flee rather than fight; carry sealed correspondence) [PF2e Lvl 5–7]
An Ancient Thing — a creature of immense age has emerged from a deep crevasse to investigate the road. It is not hostile. It is also not safe. [GM's discretion — Lvl 15+]
